q: The area (in sq. units) of the region <math><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{" separators="|"><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>y</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>:</mo><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mi> </mi><mo>≤</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>≤</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is
question: The area (in sq. units) of the region <math><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{" separators="|"><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>y</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>:</mo><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mi> </mi><mo>≤</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>≤</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is
solution:
The two given curves are y=x+2 and y=x2

To find their points of intersection, we equate the value of y to get

x2=x+2

⇒x2-x-2=0

⇒x-2x+1=0

⇒x=2 or x=-1.

The graphs of the curves are given below.


Required area is the area of the shaded portion.

Area =∫-12yline-yparaboladx
=∫-12x+2-x2dx

Using ∫xndx=xn+1n+1

=x22+ 2x-x33-12

=2+4-83-12-2+13

=6-83-12+ 2-13

=8-3-12

=92 sq units.

q: The area (in sq. units) of the smaller portion enclosed between the curves, <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn></math> and <math><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi></math>, is:

solution:
To find point of intersection, eliminate y from both the equation, we get

x2+3x-4=0

⇒ x+4 x-1=0

x=-4, x=1

Area =∫013·x·dx+∫124-x2·dx×2

=3x323201+x24-x2+2sin-1⁡x212×2

=323+2·π2-32+π3×2

=23-32+2π3×2

=123+2π3×2=13+4π3 sq. units

subject: Mathematics
q: The area of the region enclosed by the curves $y=x^2-4 x+4$ and $y^2=16-8 x$ is :
question: The area of the region enclosed by the curves $y=x^2-4 x+4$ and $y^2=16-8 x$ is :
solution: $\begin{aligned} \text { Area } & =\int_0^2\left(\sqrt{16-8 x}-\left(x^2-4 x+4\right)\right) d x \\ & \left.=\frac{-(16-8 x)^{3 / 2}}{12}-\frac{x^3}{3}+2 x^2+4 x\right]_0^2 \\ & =\frac{8}{3}\end{aligned}$

question: If the area of the bounded region <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>y</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>:</mo><mi>max</mi><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>≤</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>≤</mo><msup><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></msup><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>≤</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>≤</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><msup><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></msub><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>γ</mi></math> then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>γ</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to:
solution:
R=x,y:max0,logex≤y≤2x,12≤x≤2

So, the area of the region is

∫1222xdx-∫12ℓnxdx

=2xln21/22-xlnx-x12

=22-21/2loge2-2ln2-1

=22-2loge2-2ln2+1

∴ α=22-2,β=-2,γ=1

So, α+β-2γ2

=22-2-2-22

=-22=2

subject: Mathematics
q: Three points O0,0, Pa,a2, Q−b,b2, a>0, b>0, are on the parabola y=x2. Let S1 be the area of the region bounded by the line PQ and the parabola, and S2 be the area of the triangle OPQ. If the minimum value of S1S2 is mn, gcdm,n=1, then m+n is equal to:
question: Three points O0,0, Pa,a2, Q−b,b2, a>0, b>0, are on the parabola y=x2. Let S1 be the area of the region bounded by the line PQ and the parabola, and S2 be the area of the triangle OPQ. If the minimum value of S1S2 is mn, gcdm,n=1, then m+n is equal to:
solution:
Plotting the diagram of the given data we get,

Area of △OPQ is given by,

S2=12001aa21−bb21

⇒S2=12ab2+a2b   ...i

Now, equation of line PQ:y−a2=a2−b2a+bx−a

⇒y−a2=a−bx−a−ba

⇒y=a−bx+ab   ...ii

Area bounded by the line PQ and the parabola is given by,

S1=∫−baa−bx+ab−x2dx

⇒S1=a−bx22+abx−x33−ba

⇒S1=a−b2a+b2+aba+b−a3+b33   ...iii

⇒S1S2=a−b22+ab−a2+b2−ab3ab2

⇒S1S2=3a−b2+6ab−2a2+b2−ab3ab

⇒S1S2=3a2+b2-2ab+6ab−2a2+b2−ab3ab

⇒S1S2=3a2+3b2-6ab+6ab-2a2-2b2+2ab3ab

⇒S1S2=a2+b2+2ab3ab

⇒S1S2=13ab+ba+2

⇒S1S2min=131+1+2 as ab+ba≥2

⇒S1S2min=43

⇒m+n=7

q: If the area (in sq. units) of the region <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="{" close="}" separators="|"><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>y</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>≤</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>≤</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>≥</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>y</mi><mo>≥</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is <math><mi>a</mi><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>b</mi></math> is equal to
question: If the area (in sq. units) of the region <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="{" close="}" separators="|"><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>y</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>≤</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>≤</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>≥</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>y</mi><mo>≥</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is <math><mi>a</mi><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>b</mi></math> is equal to
solution:
The given equations are,

y2=4x & x+y=1

⇒y2=41-y

⇒y2+4y-4=0 ⇒y=22-2

∴ Required Area =∫022-21-y-y24dx=y-y22-y312022-2

=22-2-22-222-22-2312-0

=22-21-2-1-2-123=-103+823

⇒-103+823=a2+b ⇒a=83, b=-103
∴ a-b=83+103=6

q: If the area of the region <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>:</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>≤</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>≤</mo><mi>min</mi><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>6</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>-</mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is equal to _______.
question: If the area of the region <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>:</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>≤</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>≤</mo><mi>min</mi><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>6</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>-</mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is equal to _______.
solution:
Solving, y=2x and y=6x-x2

⇒2x=6x-x2

⇒4x-x2=0

⇒x4-x=0

⇒x=0,4

⇒y=0,8

⇒x,y≡0,0, 4,8

So, the required area is given by,

A=12×4×8+∫466x-x2dx

⇒A=16+3x2-x3346

⇒A=763

⇒12A=304

question: If the area of the region <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi><mo mathvariant="italic">=</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mo mathvariant="italic">(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo mathvariant="italic">,</mo><mi>y</mi><mo mathvariant="italic">)</mo><mo mathvariant="italic">:</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>y</mi><mo mathvariant="italic">-</mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo mathvariant="italic">≤</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo mathvariant="italic">≤</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>y</mi><mo mathvariant="italic">,</mo><mi>x</mi><mo mathvariant="italic">≥</mo><mi>y</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo mathvariant="italic">-</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo mathvariant="italic">-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo mathvariant="italic">,</mo></math> then the natural number <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> is equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi></math>
solution:
Given,

S=(x,y):2y-y2≤x2≤2y,x≥y,

Now plotting the diagram, we get

Now from above diagram, given region are,

2y-y2≤x2, x2≤2y and x≥y

Now the intersecting point of x2+y2-2y=0 & x=y will be 1,1 and x2=2y & x=y will be 2,2

So, required area will be,

A=∫011+1-x2-x22+∫12x-x22dx

⇒A=x-x21-x2-12·sin-1x-x3601+x22-x3612

⇒A=76-π4=n+2n+1-πx-1

So, on comparing we get, n=5

solution:
We have, dydx=2x

∴ Slope of tangent at 2,3 is

m=4

∴ Equation of tangent is y-3=4x-2

q: The area of the region $\mathrm{A}=\left\{(x, y): 4 x^{2}+y^{2} \leqslant 8\right.$ and $\left.y^{2} \leqslant 4 x\right\}$ is:
question: The area of the region $\mathrm{A}=\left\{(x, y): 4 x^{2}+y^{2} \leqslant 8\right.$ and $\left.y^{2} \leqslant 4 x\right\}$ is:
solution:
$A = \int_{0}^{2} 2\sqrt{x} \, dx + 2\int_{1}^{\sqrt{2}} \sqrt{8 - 4x^2} \, dx$
$= \dfrac{8}{3} \left( x^{\frac{3}{2}} \right) \bigg|_{0}^{1} + 4\int_{1}^{\sqrt{2}} \sqrt{2 - x^2} \, dx$
$= \dfrac{8}{3} + 4 \times \dfrac{1}{2} \left[ x\sqrt{2 - x^2} + 2\sin^{-1}\left( \dfrac{x}{\sqrt{2}} \right) \right] \bigg|_{1}^{\sqrt{2}}$
$= \dfrac{8}{3} + 2 \left[ 2 \times \dfrac{\pi}{2} - 1 - 2 \times \dfrac{\pi}{4} \right]$
$= \dfrac{8}{3} + 2\pi - 2 - \pi = \pi + \dfrac{2}{3}$ sq. units

question: Let the area of the region enclosed by the curves $y=3 x, 2 y=27-3 x$ and $y=3 x-x \sqrt{x}$ be $A$. Then $10 A$ is equal to
solution:
$y=3 x, 2 y=27-3 x \& y=3 x-x \sqrt{x}$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{A}=\int_0^3 3 \mathrm{x}-(3 \mathrm{x}-\mathrm{x} \sqrt{\mathrm{x}}) \mathrm{dx}+\int_3^9\left(\frac{27-3 \mathrm{x}}{2}-(3 \mathrm{x}-\mathrm{x} \sqrt{\mathrm{x}})\right) \mathrm{dx} \\ & \mathrm{A}=\int_0^3 \mathrm{x}^{3 / 2} \mathrm{dx}+\int_3^9 \frac{27}{2}-\frac{9 \mathrm{x}}{2}+\mathrm{x}^{3 / 2} \mathrm{dx} \\ & \mathrm{A}=\left[\frac{2 \mathrm{x}^{5 / 2}}{5}\right]_0^3+\frac{27}{2}[\mathrm{x}]_3^9-\frac{9}{2}\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^2}{2}\right]_3^9+\left[\frac{2 \mathrm{x}^{5 / 2}}{5}\right]_3^9 \\ & \mathrm{~A}=\frac{2}{5}\left(3^{5 / 2}\right)+\frac{27}{2}(6)-\frac{9}{4}(72)+\frac{2}{5}\left(9^{5 / 2}-3^{5 / 2}\right) \\ & \mathrm{A}=\frac{2}{5}\left(3^{5 / 2}\right)+81-162+\frac{2}{5} \times 3^5-\frac{2}{5} \times 3^{5 / 2} \\ & \mathrm{~A}=\frac{486}{5}-81=\frac{81}{5} \\ & 10 \mathrm{~A}=162 \\ & \text {Ans. }=(2)\end{aligned}$

q: The area of the region bounded by the curves $x+3y^2=0$ and $x+4y^2=1$ is equal to:
question: The area of the region bounded by the curves $x+3y^2=0$ and $x+4y^2=1$ is equal to:
solution:
The equations of the given curves are $x = -3y^2$ and $x = 1 - 4y^2$.

To find the points of intersection, equate the values of $x$:

$-3y^2 = 1 - 4y^2$

$y^2 = 1 \Rightarrow y = \pm 1$

The region is bounded between $y = -1$ and $y = 1$. In this interval, $1 - 4y^2 \ge -3y^2$.

The required area $A$ is given by:

$A = \int_{-1}^{1} \left( (1 - 4y^2) - (-3y^2) \right) dy$

$A = \int_{-1}^{1} (1 - y^2) dy$

Since $1 - y^2$ is an even function:

$A = 2 \int_{0}^{1} (1 - y^2) dy$

$A = 2 \left[ y - \dfrac{y^3}{3} \right]_{0}^{1}$

$A = 2 \left( 1 - \dfrac{1}{3} \right) = 2 \left( \dfrac{2}{3} \right) = \dfrac{4}{3}$

Answer: $\dfrac{4}{3}$

question:
For real numbers a, ba>b>0, let

Area x, y:x2+y2≤a2 and x2a2+y2b2≥1=30π

and

Area x, y:x2+y2≥b2 and x2a2+y2b2≤1=18π

Then the value of a-b2 is equal to _____.
solution:
We know that area of circle =πr2 (r is radius of circle) and area of ellipse =πab (where a & b are length of semi major and semi minor axis)

Now given area between circle and ellipse  ⇒πa2-πab=30π  .....(i)

And ⇒πab-πb2=18π ....(ii)

On subtracting equation (i) - (ii), we get a-b2=a2-2ab+b2=12.

q: The area (in sq. units) of the region bounded by the curve $x^{2}=4 y$ and the straight line $x=4 y-2$ is :
question: The area (in sq. units) of the region bounded by the curve $x^{2}=4 y$ and the straight line $x=4 y-2$ is :
solution: Let points of intersection of the curve and the line be $P$ and $Q$ $x^{2}=4\left(\frac{x+2}{4}\right)$ $x^{2}-x-2=0$ $x=2,-1$ Point are (2,1) and $\left(-1, \frac{1}{4}\right)$ $\text { Area }=\int_{-1}^{2}\left[\left(\frac{x+2}{4}\right)-\left(\frac{x^{2}}{4}\right)\right] d x=\left[\frac{x^{2}}{8}+\frac{1}{2} x-\frac{x^{3}}{12}\right]_{-1}^{2}$ $=\left(\frac{1}{2}+1-\frac{2}{3}\right)-\left(\frac{1}{8}-\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{12}\right)=\frac{9}{8}$

subject: Mathematics
q: The area of the region, inside the ellipse $x^{2}+4 y^{2}=4$ and outside the region bounded by the curves $y=|x|-1$ and $y=1-|x|$, is :
question: The area of the region, inside the ellipse $x^{2}+4 y^{2}=4$ and outside the region bounded by the curves $y=|x|-1$ and $y=1-|x|$, is :
solution:
Ellipse: $\frac{x^2}{4} + y^2 = 1$ with $a = 2$, $b = 1$. Area $= \pi ab = 2\pi$.
Curves $y = |x| - 1$ and $y = 1 - |x|$ intersect at $x = \pm 1$, forming a rhombus with vertices $(0, 1)$, $(1, 0)$, $(0, -1)$, $(-1, 0)$.
Vertices $(0, \pm 1)$ lie on ellipse; $(\pm 1, 0)$ are inside ellipse.
Area of rhombus $= \frac{1}{2} \times 2 \times 2 = 2$.
Required area $= 2\pi - 2 = 2(\pi - 1)$.
